generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision analog/digital temperature sensor measuring internal environment of the incubator box. Deployed to capture thermal gradients for model calibration and real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision analog/digital temperature sensor measuring internal environment of the incubator box. Deployed to capture thermal gradients for model calibration and real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision analog/digital temperature sensor measuring ambient conditions outside the insulated styrofoam box. Provides environmental context for thermal model validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataStream1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of analog temperature readings (likely in °C) from InternalTemperatureSensor1. Transmitted via local network (Ethernet/Wi-Fi) to Raspberry Pi controller with high-resolution and time-sensitive requirements."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureDataStream2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of analog temperature readings (likely in °C) from InternalTemperatureSensor2. Transmitted via local network (Ethernet/Wi-Fi) to Raspberry Pi controller with high-resolution and time-sensitive requirements."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of analog temperature readings (likely in °C) from ExternalTemperatureSensor. Transmitted via local network (Ethernet/Wi-Fi) to Raspberry Pi controller for environmental context."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal dynamics model with two free parameters representing simplified lumped capacitance behavior of the incubator system. Used for initial predictive simulations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity thermal dynamics model with four free parameters demonstrating superior predictive accuracy for thermal inertia. Captures nuanced physical behaviors for precise control."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, ModelPredictiveControlEnabler, OptimizationEnabler
]

instance RawSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed analog temperature readings from all sensors before noise filtering and normalization. Includes raw time-series data from internal and external temperature sensors."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataStream1, InternalTemperatureDataStream2, ExternalTemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Filtered and normalized temperature readings after preprocessing steps. Includes noise reduction and value normalization for integration into simulation environment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, StateEstimationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database of temperature measurements spanning operational history. Used for long-term trend analysis and model validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedSensorData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Calibration algorithm employing least-squares optimization or Bayesian inference to adapt model parameters (e.g., thermal conductivity) using real-time data. Ensures predictive fidelity between DT and PT."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance DataPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Noise filtering and normalization algorithms applied to raw sensor data before integration into Digital Twin models. Ensures data quality for accurate simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService, FaultDiagnosticsService
]

instance FaultDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection algorithm using statistical thresholds or machine learning classifiers trained on calibrated Digital Twin data. Identifies sensor failures or thermal anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDiagnosticsService, SafetyValidationService
]

instance ModelPredictiveControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Supervisory controller implementing model-predictive control (MPC) or PID tuning. Generates control signals optimized by Digital Twin predictions for real-time adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAssistanceService
]

instance OptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Gradient-based or metaheuristic optimization (e.g., genetic algorithms) refining system parameters (e.g., heating profiles) for energy efficiency. Validated against real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationRecommendationService
]

instance StateEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimator generating digital replica of the Physical Twin's state by merging sensor data with dynamic models. Enables real-time monitoring and predictive simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveSimulationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous ingestion of sensor data to generate a digital replica of the Physical Twin's state. Uses calibration algorithms to reconcile model parameters with observed data."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight, FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of dynamic models (differential equations) to simulate thermal response under current or hypothetical conditions. Supports what-if analysis and future state predictions."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureTrajectoryInsight, ParameterImpactInsight
]

instance FaultDiagnosticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Identification of anomalies or failures by cross-referencing simulated and real-world data. Generates root cause analysis for detected deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides RootCauseInsight, CorrectiveActionInsight
]

instance ControlAssistanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implementation of supervisory controllers to suggest adjustments (e.g., heating duty cycles) for improved efficiency or safety. Validates control policies against real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlPolicyInsight
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven suggestions for parameter adjustments or corrective actions to maintain performance. Optimizes heating profiles and system parameters for energy efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationInsight, EnergyEfficiencyInsight
]

instance SafetyValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitoring of Physical Twin's adherence to operational constraints (e.g., temperature thresholds). Triggers alerts or automated interventions when violations are detected."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivery of advanced visualizations (3D thermal maps, time-series plots) to engineers or operators. Enables intuitive interpretation of system behavior and historical data review."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalMapInsight, InteractiveDashboardInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of the Physical Twin's operational state via real-time sensor data and model reconciliation. Provides confidence measures derived from model calibration."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of deviations from optimal or safe operating conditions through cross-referencing of simulated and real-world data."
]

    base:desc "Predictive insights into future temperature states based on calibrated models and environmental forecasts. Enables proactive adjustments."
]

]

    base:desc "Diagnostic information identifying the underlying causes of detected anomalies or failures in the Physical Twin."
]

    base:desc "Suggested adjustments to mitigate detected faults or deviations, derived from Digital Twin's predictive capabilities."
]

instance ControlPolicyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimal control policies generated by model-based optimization, validated against real-time data before deployment."
]

instance SafetyViolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of operational constraint violations (e.g., temperature thresholds) requiring immediate attention."
]

]

instance OptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for parameter adjustments to improve system efficiency or performance."
]

]

]

]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatedBedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary heating element dynamically adjusting temperature within the insulated styrofoam box. Governed by software controller interfacing with the Physical Twin via embedded communication protocols."
]

    base:desc "Mechanical component ensuring uniform heat distribution inside the incubator box. Operates under control signals from the Digital Twin's optimization algorithms."
]

    base:desc "Thermal insulation container minimizing heat loss and maintaining internal temperature stability. Acts as the primary enclosure for the incubator system."
]

    base:desc "Embedded controller interfacing with sensors and actuators via I/O protocols (GPIO, I2C). Acts as the cyber-physical interface for closed-loop regulation."
]

instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains InsulatedStyrofoamBox, HeatedBedActuator, FanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor, RaspberryPiController

instance StateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates digital replica of the Physical Twin's state by merging sensor data with dynamic models. Enables real-time monitoring and predictive simulations."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via **command signals** generated by algorithms (e.g., supervisory controllers, optimizers, or fault detectors) to regulate the incubator’s temperature. The **command types** include **heating activation/deactivation** (via the heatbed) and **fan control** (to distribute heat uniformly). The **control message format** is not explicitly detailed but likely follows a **binary or analog signal protocol** (e.g., PWM for the heatbed or ON/OFF for the fan). **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not described, but the system implies **closed-loop feedback** where the DT continuously monitors PT performance (via temperature sensors) and adjusts commands dynamically. **Conflict resolution** is handled implicitly by the calibration algorithms, which ensure the DT’s models align with the PT’s observed behavior, preventing divergent control actions. **Command execution confirmation** is inferred through the **monitoring loop**, where the DT verifies the PT’s response (e.g., temperature stabilization) before further adjustments."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system operates with a **real-time synchronization paradigm**, where data from the Physical Twin (PT) is continuously collected via sensors (e.g., temperature sensors in the incubator case study) and fed into the DT framework. The temporal granularity of data acquisition is **high-frequency**, though exact sampling rates are not specified in the documents. The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update its models (e.g., differential equations for dynamic behavior) with the latest sensor data, ensuring the digital representation aligns with the PT's current state. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **control loop of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS)**, where deviations in the PT (e.g., temperature fluctuations) must be detected and corrected promptly. The DT's predictive and monitoring capabilities rely on this **tight coupling between real-time data ingestion and model updates**, enabling dynamic adjustments to the PT's operation (e.g., via supervisory controllers or human intervention)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **modular, service-oriented architecture** (as depicted in Fig. 1), where key components include:
  1. **Data Acquisition Layer**: Sensors (e.g., temperature sensors in the incubator) stream real-time data to the DT.
  2. **Model Layer**: Contains **cyber-physical models** (e.g., differential equations for dynamics) and **environmental models**, updated via **calibration algorithms** to minimize parameter estimation errors.
  3. **Algorithm Layer**: Hosts **fault detectors**, **supervisory controllers**, and **optimizers** that process data and models to generate actionable insights or corrections.
  4. **Monitoring Layer**: Continuously evaluates PT performance against safety and optimality thresholds, triggering interventions (e.g., human or automated) when violations occur.
  The orchestration relies on **closed-loop feedback** between the PT and DT, with **resource management** implicitly handled by the integration of these layers. The system does not explicitly describe **distributed orchestration patterns** (e.g., microservices or edge computing) but assumes a **unified control plane** for coordination. Integration of components follows a **tightly coupled** approach, where data flows sequentially from sensors → calibration → monitoring → actuation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the provided context includes the following phases: **(1) Modeling Phase**: Abstract representations of the Physical Twin (PT) are created at varying levels of abstraction, such as CAD models (e.g., geometric and structural details of a car chassis) or dynamic models (e.g., differential equations for simulating forces and movement). These models may range from static representations (e.g., shapes, sizes) to dynamic simulations (e.g., physics-based behavior under operating conditions). **(2) Integration Phase**: Models are integrated into the DT framework alongside real-time **Data** (collected via sensors from the PT) and **Algorithms** (e.g., calibration, fault detection, state estimation). **(3) Deployment Phase**: The DT is deployed to monitor and interact with the PT, where it continuously updates models via **calibration algorithms** to align with the PT’s current state. **(4) Operational Phase**: The DT operates in tandem with the PT, enabling real-time **monitoring** (e.g., safety checks via supervisory controllers) and **optimization** (e.g., adjusting parameters to maintain optimal performance). **(5) Evolution Phase**: The DT evolves through iterative updates, where deviations or violations detected by monitors trigger corrective actions (e.g., human intervention or automated adjustments to the PT). **Lifecycle Governance**: The process emphasizes retaining models post-deployment to ensure continuity between design, simulation, and operational phases, with a focus on **reconfigurability** (adapting to environmental changes) and **predictability** (simulating PT behavior under current conditions)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity in the Digital Twin (DT) is determined by the **complexity and accuracy of simulation models**, as demonstrated in the incubator case study where the **four-parameter thermal model** outperformed the two-parameter variant in predicting thermal inertia. Validation and verification (V&V) methods include **experimental comparison** between simulated and real-world data (e.g., incubator temperature logs) to quantify discrepancies. Uncertainty sources are attributed to **sensor inaccuracies**, **environmental disturbances** (e.g., external heat sources), and **model simplifications**. Error detection mechanisms rely on **calibration algorithms** that adjust model parameters (e.g., thermal conductivity) to minimize prediction errors. Quality assurance processes involve **iterative refinement** of models based on empirical data, ensuring the DT remains aligned with the Physical Twin (PT). The DT framework also employs **fault detection algorithms** to identify anomalies in sensor data or model outputs, triggering corrective actions (e.g., controller adjustments) to maintain system integrity."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) for the incubator relies on a **cyber-physical system (CPS) architecture** where the **Raspberry Pi** serves as the central node connecting the PT (sensors/actuators) to the DT’s computational environment. The **network topology** is likely a **star configuration**, with the Raspberry Pi acting as the hub for sensor data aggregation and actuator control. **Communication protocols** are not explicitly specified but may include **serial (UART), I2C, or SPI** for sensor-actuator communication, alongside **Ethernet/Wi-Fi** for cloud-edge integration if applicable. **Bandwidth requirements** are minimal for this use case but must support **real-time sensor data streams** (e.g., 1–10 Hz updates for temperature sensors). **Latency requirements** are critical for control loops, though exact thresholds are unspecified. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not detailed, but the system implies **fault-tolerant design** via calibration algorithms that correct model discrepancies. **Security protocols** are not mentioned, but standard practices (e.g., encrypted communication, access controls) would be expected in a production deployment."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) for the incubator is deployed on a **Raspberry Pi**, which serves as the **edge computing platform** hosting the DT’s models, algorithms, and real-time monitoring logic. The **computational requirements** are lightweight, focusing on **model calibration, simulation, and control execution**, with no mention of high-performance computing (HPC) needs. **Storage requirements** are minimal, primarily for logging sensor data and model parameters. **Scalability mechanisms** are not addressed, as the use case is a **single-node, small-scale CPS**. The **deployment model** is **on-premise/edge**, with no cloud dependency implied. **Availability/reliability assurance** relies on the Raspberry Pi’s stability and the **closed-loop calibration** process, which dynamically adjusts the DT to maintain synchronization with the PT. No redundancy or failover mechanisms are specified."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system integrates **safety-critical monitoring** through **monitors** that check whether the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its Physical Twin (PT) are performing **safely and optimally** (as depicted in Figure 1). The system employs **calibration algorithms** to update models with the best parameter estimates, ensuring accurate representation of the PT's state. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied by the DT's ability to detect violations (e.g., deviations from optimal/safe operation) and trigger **human or algorithmic interventions** to modify the PT's behavior. While the documents do not specify explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, access control, or authentication systems), the DT framework inherently relies on **secure data transmission** from sensors (monitoring the PT) to the cyber components (models, algorithms). The **risk assessment** is embedded in the monitoring phase, where deviations from expected behavior are flagged for corrective action. No explicit mention of compliance with data protection regulations (e.g., GDPR), consent management, or data anonymization is provided in the documents."
]
